# Source

The `agent-*.png` sprites in this directory are **composed from real layers
of the Universal LPC Spritesheet Character Generator** — they are NOT
generated/original art and must never be relabeled as such.

- Upstream repository:
  <https://github.com/LiberatedPixelCup/Universal-LPC-Spritesheet-Character-Generator>
- Upstream commit used: `8d9b9bdbaaee090cc2bc12e4c497552490fbc9e2`
- Composer script: `tools/compose-lpc-agents.mjs` in this example

## What the composer does

For every agent role it:

1. loads the `sit.png` sheet of each chosen layer (body, legs, clothes,
   necktie, head, eyes, glasses, hair) from a local checkout of the
   generator (`spritesheets/...`);
2. recolors the layers with the generator's own palette ramps
   (`palette_definitions/{body,hair,cloth,eye}`);
3. composites them in the generator's zPos order on the south-facing
   chair-sit frame (sit sheet row 2, column 2);
4. draws a simple office chair BEHIND the figure — the chair pixels are
   original to this repo, not LPC;
5. crops the seated bust at the desk cut line (the workstation desk tile
   covers the lap) and writes a 2-frame strip (frames identical — agents
   sit still).

## Reproducing

```sh
git clone https://github.com/LiberatedPixelCup/Universal-LPC-Spritesheet-Character-Generator ~/tmp/ulpc
git -C ~/tmp/ulpc checkout 8d9b9bdbaaee090cc2bc12e4c497552490fbc9e2
ULPC_DIR=~/tmp/ulpc npm run compose:lpc
```

The composed PNGs are committed, so this is only needed to change the
characters. `ATTRIBUTIONS.md` (per-source-file authors, licenses, URLs) is
regenerated by the same script from the generator's `CREDITS.csv`.

See `ATTRIBUTIONS.md` for the credits and `LICENSES.md` for the licensing of
these sprites (they are NOT covered by the repository's MIT license).
